title: Post-spinal headache documentation

examination.text: Supports documenting the dural-puncture context, headache timing and features, associated symptoms, focused history and examination, and atypical or severe neurological features without automatically diagnosing PDPH.
examination.rationale: The exact ASA section supports documenting the puncture context, headache timing and features, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, and focused assessment without automatically diagnosing PDPH.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting the dural-puncture context, headache timing and features, associated symptoms, focused history and examination, and atypical or severe neurological features without automatically diagnosing PDPH.

source.title: Statement on Post-Dural Puncture Headache Management

exact_section.heading: PDPH Definition and PDPH Diagnosis
exact_section.locator: official statement, PDPH Definition and PDPH Diagnosis paragraphs

examination.text: Supports documented follow-up until symptom resolution, brief neurological assessment, and verbal or written contact advice as clinician-completed actions without generating treatment.
examination.rationale: The follow-up section supports recording follow-up and contact advice only when actually arranged or discussed by the clinician.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documented follow-up until symptom resolution, brief neurological assessment, and verbal or written contact advice as clinician-completed actions without generating treatment.

source_limitations: Exact spinal or epidural procedure, puncture recognition, postural character, neurological findings, blood pressure and pregnancy context, differential diagnosis, imaging, consultation, treatment, response, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
